name: Belvis de Monroy – circular via the Berrocal Hermitage and San Francisco Convent
name_original: Belvis de Monroy – circular por la Ermita del Berrocal y Convento de San Francisco
region_intro: Hiking around Belvís De Monroy offers diverse landscapes in the Campo Arañuelo region of Extremadura, Spain. The area is characterized by its unique dehesa ecosystem, featuring oak trees and open grazing lands. Proximity to the Tagus River and the Valdecañas Reservoir provides scenic water views and routes along their banks. Rolling hills and varied terrain are present, suitable for different hiking levels. Best hiking trails around Belvís De Monroy The most popular hiking route is Belvis de Monroy – circular via the Berrocal Hermitage and San Francisco Convent, an easy 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This route passes historical sites and offers views of the local dehesa. Another top favourite among local hikers is Acueducto de las Herrerías loop from Campillo de Deleitosa, a moderate 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This trail features varied terrain with some elevation gain, leading through the natural surroundings. Local hikers also love the Navalmoral de la Mata loop from Navalmoral de la Mata, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Belvís De Monroy is defined by its dehesa landscapes, the Tagus River, and the Valdecañas Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle walks to more demanding climbs. The routes in Belvís De Monroy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Belvís De Monroy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Belvís De Monroy?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;7&lt;/strong&gt; distinct hiking routes available around Belvís De Monroy, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more moderate challenges through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Belvís De Monroy?
A: Yes, for an easy and family-friendly option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990750287/belvis-de-monroy-circular-via-the-berrocal-hermitage-and-san-francisco-convent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvis de Monroy – circular via the Berrocal Hermitage and San Francisco Convent&lt;/a&gt;. This 6.8 km trail is part of the historical Senda de los Frailes, passing significant sites and offering gentle terrain suitable for all ages.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking here?
A: The region is characterized by its unique &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; ecosystem, featuring expansive oak woodlands and open grazing lands. You'll also find scenic routes along the Tagus River and the Valdecañas Reservoir, offering beautiful water views. Keep an eye out for the impressive 'Alcornoque de la Dehesa', a 250-year-old cork oak with a 26-meter canopy.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. Many trails incorporate historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990750287/belvis-de-monroy-circular-via-the-berrocal-hermitage-and-san-francisco-convent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvis de Monroy – circular via the Berrocal Hermitage and San Francisco Convent&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, visits the Convent of San Francisco, a site of historical significance. You can also explore the remains of the Roman marble &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views of the Valdecañas reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990750287/belvis-de-monroy-circular-via-the-berrocal-hermitage-and-san-francisco-convent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvis de Monroy – circular via the Berrocal Hermitage and San Francisco Convent&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 6.8 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31868546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.9 km trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Belvís De Monroy?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Belvís De Monroy. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes, especially the dehesa, are at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if hiking during that season.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Belvís De Monroy?
A: The hiking routes in Belvís De Monroy are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the historical points of interest, and the scenic views of the dehesa and the Valdecañas Reservoir.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While most routes are easy to moderate, some trails offer more significant elevation gain and distance. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de las Herrerías loop from Campillo de Deleitosa&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.4 km path with varied terrain and notable ascents.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting directly from Belvís de Monroy or nearby towns, you can often find public parking in the village centers. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the hiking trails in the Belvís De Monroy area, especially those through the dehesa and open countryside. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near wildlife, and respect local regulations.

Q: What are some notable attractions to visit besides hiking?
A: Beyond hiking, you can visit the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt;, which dominates the town's skyline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaraz Bridge over the Tagus River&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views and is worth a stop.

Q: Are there options for longer hikes in the region?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer excursion, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31866335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higuera de Albalat loop from Romangordo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17 km trail that allows for deeper exploration of the area's natural beauty and takes several hours to complete.
tips: Section that passes through the Laguna de la Feria, Convent of San Francisco del Berrocal and the Ermita del Berrocal.
